State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission,
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662         322-5660           322-5901          322-6441         322-6444

October 19, 1979


Honorable Frank A. Gonzales, Mayor
City of Colton
Colton Civic Center
650 North La Cadena Drive
Colton, CA 92324

Dear Mayor Gonzales:

In your letter of September 28, 1979, you inquire about
a matter arising under the disqualification provisions (Government Code Sections 87100, et seq.) of the Political Reform
Act ("the Act"). Because, under the Act, opinions and advice
are issued only in response to inquiries from persons, or on
behalf of persons, whose duties are the subject of the inquiries,
your request for a formal opinion in this matter is denied.
The following discussion is provided for information only.

You ask specifically whether a city councilmember should
be disqualified from participation in city council personnel
actions concerning a discharged city employee who had contributed to the councilmember's 1978 campaign committee.

Government Code Section 87100 prohibits any public
official at any level of state or local government from
making, participating in the making or in any way attempting
to use his official position to influence a governmental
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a
financial interest.

Government Code Section 87103 states that a public
official has "financial interest" in a decision if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the
public generally, on: "(c) any source of income...."

The terms of Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103
are substantially incorporated by Section 4 of City of Colton
Resolution No. 3623, adopting a conflict of interest code
applicable to members of the city council pursuant to the
Political Reform Act of 1974.

Honorable Frank A. Gonzales, Mayor
October 19, 1979
Page Two


     "Income" is specifically defined by Government Code
Section 82030(b)(1) to exclude campaign contributions required
to be reported under Chapter 4 of the Act.

     Assuming that the employee's contribution to the council-
man is a contribution within the meaning of the Act, it
would not constitute "income" to the councilmember. Accordingly,
the Act would not require the councilmember to disqualify
himself from participating in decisions affecting the employee.

     Even though the councilmember would not be required to
disqualify himself under the Act, please be advised that the
Supreme Court is presently considering the question whether
city councilmembers' participation in a zoning decision
directly affecting contributors to their campaigns may be
said to create an appearance of bias sufficient to provide a
basis for overturning a decision under Code of Civil Procedure
1094.5.  Woodland Hills Residents Ass'n., Inc., et al. v.
Los Angeles City Council (L.A. No. 31133).

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        [signature]
                                        Thomas M. Sobel
                                        Chief, Legal Division

CITY OF COLTON
SEPTEMBER 28, 1979

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 Kay Street
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

Enclosed please find copies of original documents on file in the
office of the City Clerk.

Minutes from City Council Meeting, July 17, 1979
Minutes from City Council Meeting, September 18, 1979
City of Colton Resolution No. 3650
Committee Campaign Statement dated October 24, 1978,
for L. E. Temby.

This matter pertains to Council Member Leonard E. Temby's voting on
personnel matters relating to the discharge of City employee, Jerry
Young, as outlined on pages 3 and 4 of Minutes dated July 17, 1979,
and Mrs. Young's reinstatement, as outlined on page 4, 5 and 6 of
Minutes dated September 18, 1979. Mr. Young was a monetary contributor to Councilman Temby's campaign, as indicated on page 4 of the
Committee Campaign Statement.

City of Colton's Ordinance No. 1122, Section 18, Solicitation of
Contributions, prohibits any City employee, or any candidate for
any City office to receive contributions on political service,
whether voluntary or involuntary, for any political purpose whatsoever.

The question is whether or not there is a Conflict of Interest involved here. Should Councilman Temby have participated, or abstained
from voting, in the decision making process regarding employee, Jerry
Young.

A determination and ruling on this matter will be greatly appreciated.

Very truly yours,

FRANK A. GONZALES
Mayor

COLTON CIVIC CENTER • 650 NORTH LA CADENA DRIVE • COLTON, CALIFORNIA 92324 • (714) 825-3110

SISTER CITY • CANANEA, SONORA, MEXICO

COUNCIL MEETING

JULY 17, 1979

A regular meeting of the City Council of the City of Colton was held on the above
given date at 7:30 p.m., in the Council Chambers of City Hall, Mayor Gonzales pre-
siding.

Invocation was offered by Dr. Robert Shiffleth of the World Prophetic Ministry.

Mayor Gonzales led the group in the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag.

ROLL CALL:

Council Members present were Cisneros, Rehrer, Temby, and Mayor Gonzales. Also
present were City Manager Calabrese, City Attorney Edwards, and City Clerk Ramos.
Absent was Councilman Hayes due to illness.

CONSENT CALENDAR:

Minutes
    July 3, 1979
    July 9, 1979
    July 11, 1979

Warrants
    25555, 25001 through 26028
    18959 through 19102
    July 13, 1979 inclusive

Bids
    Advertise for Bids to construct basketball court, barbecue pit, and shelter
    at Veterans Park. (State funded)

Fleming Park
    Chamber of Commerce, Women's Division, for Little Miss Colton Contest, Saturday,
    July 28, 1979, at 4:00 p.m.

Dance Permit

Mr. Jose Prieto, 1110 Pennsylvania Avenue, Colton, representing the Trabajadores
Unidos, requested a dance permit for Saturday, August 4 and 11, 1979, from 8:00
p.m. to 12:00 p.m., and Saturday, September 1, 1979, from 8:00 p.m. to 12:00
p.m., and Friday, Saturday and Sunday, September 14, 15 and 16, 1979, from 8:00
p.m. to 12:00 p.m.

Police Chief Phil Elliott stated that he had been made aware of the request for the
dance permit verbally, however, he had not received a copy of the permit as yet.

City Clerk Ramos advised that Mr. Prieto had completed a dance permit application
in her office this afternoon, and it is now being processed.

Moved by Councilman Rehrer, seconded by Councilwoman Cisneros, approving Dance Permit
application requested by Jose Prieto for time and dates as outlined, subject to rati-
fication by the Chief of Police. Unanimous vote.

Moved by Councilwoman Cisneros, seconded by Councilman Rehrer, to approve the Consent Calendar. Vote was unanimous.

PLANNING COMMISSION REPORTS AND RECOMMENDATIONS:

Conditional Use Permits
P. C. Res. No. 919

Planning Director Gonzales advised that the applicant, Robertson's Ready Mix, has requested that the matter concerning their request for a Conditional Use Permit to screen and haul sand from the Santa Ana riverbed, which is being continued from a previous meeting, be continued to the next regular Council Meeting, Tuesday, August 7, 1979.

Mr. Gonzales stated the applicant has been meeting with neighbors in the surrounding area in an effort to work out several problems.

On Council consensus, Planning Commission Resolution No. 919, Conditional Use Permit application, Index No. C-5-79, was continued to the next regular Council Meeting, August 7, 1979.

Conditional Use Permits
P. C. Res. No. 922

Mr. Gonzales reported on a Conditional Use Permit application requested by Edward G. Hill to construct a 97-space mobile home park, including a number of over belt areas, a swimming pool with restroom and cabana area, with every mobile home backing onto a green belt area, on property located on the northeast corner of Pepper and San Bernardino Avenues, Index No. C-7-79.

Mr. Gonzales described the surrounding zoning and uses, stating the proposed project is consistent with the existing zoning. Mr. Gonzales advised the Planning Commission held a public hearing on this matter, July 10, 1979, and recommend approval of the Conditional Use Permit application subject to 13 conditions, as listed in Planning Commission Resolution No. 922.

Mayor Gonzales asked if anyone had questions or comments on this matter.

Moved by Councilman Rehrer, seconded by Councilwoman Cisneros, to approve Planning Commission Resolution No. 922, Index No. C-7-79. Unanimous vote.

CITY MANAGER'S REPORTS AND RECOMMENDATIONS:

Governmental Agencies
County Solid Waste Advisory Council

City Manager Calabrese presented Resolution No. 3924 to the Council Members for their consideration and approval indicating the City's approval of an amendment to the County's Solid Waste Management Master Plan providing for the limited expansion of the Heaps Peak Refuse Disposal Site.

Councilman Rehrer questioned why the City has to send the County a resolution first.

Mr. Calabrese stated the Master Plan cannot be amended until a two-thirds majority of the Cities involved agree and approve the amendment.

- 2 -

After additional discussion, moved by Councilman Rehrer, seconded by Councilman
Temby, to approve Resolution No. 3924. Unanimous vote.

RESOLUTION NO. 3924 - RESOLUTION OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF COLTON APPROVING
PLAN AMENDMENT OF THE SOLID WASTE MANAGEMENT PLAN FOR SAN
BERNARDINO COUNTY TO PROVIDE FOR THE LIMITED EXPANSION OF HEAPS
PEAK REFUSE SITE LOCATED BETWEEN RUNNING SPRINGS AND SKY FOREST
ON STATE HIGHWAY 18.

Parcel Maps

Mr. Calabrese presented Parcel Map 5321 concerning a four-lot parcel of land located
between Fur and Mill Streets, west of Pennsylvania Avenue in the City of Colton, and
Staff recommends acceptance of the additional dedication for street purposes.

Mr. Calabrese referred to the displayed parcel map, showing the location of the four-
lot parcel on the map.

Moved by Councilman Rehrer, seconded by Councilman Temby, to accept Parcel Map
5321. Vote was unanimous.

Hutton Center
Swimming Pool

Mr. Calabrese referred to a memorandum dated July 12, 1979, from Building & Safety
Director, John Twiss, and a status report on the construction of the swimming pool
in Municipal Park for Council's information.

ORAL COMMUNICATIONS:

Mr. Frank W. Smith, 270 West F Street, Colton, spoke to the Council Members with
regard to the ever-continuing problems with the Hal Kelley Grain & Milling Company
and controlling their operations, and also the Union Pacific Railroad trains noise
and disturbance within the residential areas of Colton during the early morning
hours.

The City Attorney was directed to write letters to both the Hal Kelley Grain &
Milling Company and the Union Pacific Railroad, advising them of these complaints
and requesting their assistance and support in alleviating these problems.

Mr. Frank Rebollo, General Manager of the Chamber of Commerce, spoke to the Council
Members regarding the Little Miss Colton contest scheduled for Saturday, July 28,
1979, and advised that a letter will be sent to all officials regarding "Colton
Days".

Personnel

Councilman Temby stated he would like a legal opinion with regard to the disciplinary
action taken on employee, Jerry Young.

City Attorney Edwards stated that Mr. Young had been discharged by the City Manager,
effective July 5, 1979, and Mr. Young had subsequently filed an Appeal on this action
with the City Clerk. Mr. Edwards stated that the Personnel Officer has ruled that Mr.
Young is not, as an unclassified employee, eligible to file an Appeal or have a hear-
ing on this matter, however, it is his opinion, that under the ordinance, Mr. Young

- 3 -

is in the competitive service and it would be proper for this matter to be considered by the Civil Service Board.

Moved by Councilman Temby, seconded by Councilman Rehrer, that the City Council request the Civil Service Board to hold a hearing and make recommendations as to the discipline imposed on Jorry Young, July 5, 1979, by the City Manager, the recommendations to include its opinion as to the severity of the discipline.

Mayor Gonzales stated he is in agreement with the decision of the Personnel Officer, in that due to the fact that Mr. Young's position is of an unclassified status and he is of management personnel, he is not eligible to file an Appeal or entitled to a hearing on this matter.

After additional discussion between Councilman Rehrer and Mayor Gonzales, the Mayor requested a Roll Call Vote on the motion:

AYES:     Three; Councilmen Cisneros, Rehrer, Temby.
NOES:     One; Mayor Gonzales.
ABSENT:   One; Councilman Hayes.
Motion carried.

Civil Service System
Ordinance No. 1122

Councilman Rehrer stated he would also like to bring up a matter, relating to Ordinance No. 1122, Section 6, paragraph E, which he read as follows:

"The positions of Administrative Assistant, Assistant to the City Manager, Auditor-Analyst, Cemetery Assistant, City Manager, Collections Supervisor, Director of Community Services, Director of Finance, Director of Planning and Special Services, Finance Assistant, General Services Supervisor, Parks and Cemetery Superintendent, Personnel Assistant and Streets and Sanitation Superintendent."

Councilman Rehrer continued that these particular employees are unclassified under the City's present Ordinance, and he would like to make a motion that the City Council instruct the City Attorney to draw up an amendment to this ordinance and place everyone under the Civil Service ruling, with the exception of the position of City Manager.

City Manager Calabrese advised that this is a Meet and Confer item, and suggested that it be referred to the employee associations first.

City Attorney Edwards advised that he has already prepared an ordinance and it has a number, and if the Council Members liked, he would get them a copy of it. Mr. Edwards said the ordinance is ready for introduction, and if Councilman Rehrer so desires, he can do so tonight.

Mr. Edwards presented Ordinance No. 1566, stating it does accomplish what Councilman Rehrer has mentioned and also contains a provision with regard to the procedure of appointment of department heads, with ratification by the City Council, and lack of ratification by the City Council on non-department head appointments.

- 4 -

City Attorney Edwards continued he believed the Council Members received copies of
his memo on this subject, dated July 3, 1979, and there is another memo dated July
17, 1979, which perhaps has not as yet reached the Council Members. Mr. Edwards
said in its first draft form, it was submitted july 3, 1979, then in the form as
it is this evening, it was attached to a memorandum dated today.

Mayor Gonzales stated this is the first time he has knowledge of the proposed Ordinance, and asked to see a copy of it.

Mayor Gonzales stated it is his understanding that before the City Council can take
action on a change involving personnel rules and regulations, it would be considered
a Meet and Confer item and should be brought before the employee associations.

Discussion then followed between Councilman Rehrer and Mayor Gonzales regarding the
proposed amendment, Ordinance No. 1566, the existing Ordinance, No. 1122; the Meet
and Confer process, and the City's Personnel System.

Mr. Harold Berkcbile, representing the Fire Chapter of the San Bernardino Public Employees Association, stated this is the first time, as far as he knows, that anyone
has mentioned changing the City ordinance, and he was sure that no one else here knew
about it.

Councilman Rehrer then spoke to Mr. Berkebile advising the Ordinance at the present
time reads that there are unclassified employees within the City, and these employees
who are unclassified are not covered by all of the rules that a Civil Service employee
is covered by. Mr. Rehrer continued what he is trying to do is amend the present Ordinance so that those particular employees will have that protection, under the Civil
Service rules, by making all of the positions classified, with the exception of the
position of City Manager. Mr. Rehrer concluded he is talking about the people who,
at the present time, are unclassified, and to bring them under the umbrella of Civil
Service for their protection.

Mr. Berkebile concluded he is only saying that, as far as he knows, the employee
associations do not know anything about this matter, and it was the City Council
that approved and adopted the existing Ordinance anyway.

After additional discussion, Councilman Rehrer stated he would withdraw his motion
to adopt Ordinance No. 1566, as this was probably out of order, however, he would
like to introduce Ordinance No. 1566 for consideration and discussion between the
City and the employee associations, with their recommendations to be sent back to
the City Council for action at that time.

Mr. Calabrese then spoke with regard to Ordinance No. 1122, the City's Personnel
Rules and Regulations, and the Civil Service System, as it pertained to the action
taken by the City Council regarding the Appeal of Jerry Young, and requested that
the City Clerk read the motion as made by Councilman Temby, which the City Clerk
read in full.

City Attorney Edwards then spoke with regard to the functions of the Civil Service
Board, reading Section 5, subsection c, as follows:

"The function of the Board shall be when requested by the Council or the
City Manager, the Civil Service Board shall hold hearings and make recommendations on any matter of personnel administration, within the limits of
a request of the City Council or the City Manager."

- 5 -

City Attorney Edwards concluded the motion made by Councilman Temby falls within
this category, therefore, it is a hearing conducted by the Board on a matter at
the specific direction of the City Council.

Mr. Calabrese responded then, because of that, he intends to go to the District Attorney, since there was a contribution by Mr. Young to Councilman Temby, a political
contribution, which was, as he believes, the largest individual contribution, and he
would also like the District Attorney to review Ordinance No. 1122 as it pertains to
receiving contributions from City employees by political candidates.

Councilwoman Cisneros asked if that would mean all City employees, even the City
Manager?

Mr. Calabrese responded it applies to everybody that contributes to a municipal
election.

ADJOURNMENT:

Moved by Councilman Rehrer, seconded by Councilwoman Cisneros and unanimously passed,
Mayor Gonzales adjourned the meeting at 8:30 p.m., to meet for an Adjourned Regular
Meeting on Monday, July 23, 1979, at 6:00 p.m. in the Council Chambers of City Hall.


___________________________
HELEN A. RAMOS
City Clerk
City of Colton


- 6 -

COUNCIL MEETING

SEPTEMBER 18, 1979

A regular meeting of the City Council of the City of Colton was held on the above
given date at 7:30 p.m., in the Council Chambers of City Hall, Mayor Gonzales
presiding.

Mayor Gonzales led the group in the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag.

Invocation was offered by Councilman Leroy Rehrer.

ROLL CALL:

Council Members present were Hayes, Cisneros, Rehrer, Temby, and Mayor Gonzales.
Also present were Acting City Manager Elliott, City Attorney Edwards, and City
Clerk Ramos.

CONSENT CALENDAR:

Minutes
    September 4, 1979

Warrants
    30483, 30485, 30710 through 30926
    10036, 10488 through 10644, 19104
    September 14, 1979, Inclusive

Agreements
    Joint Response Agreement with the City of Loma Linda.

Bids
    Authorization to advertise for Cab and Chassis, Electric Department.
    Authorization to advertise for 27,500 feet of Underground Cable, Electric
    Department.
    Authorization to advertise for Street Light Standards and Fixtures, Electric
    Department.
    Authorization to advertise for Monitor Defibrillator, Fire Department.
    Award of Bids, Transformers - RTE Corporation, $6,424.; Wire - Consolidated
    Electrical Distributors, $5,290.57.

Assessments
    Authorization for Appraisal of Property, Assessment District No. 78-2.

Claims
    Virginia Christensen versus City of Colton.  Recommend denial.

Moved by Councilman Hayes, seconded by Councilwoman Cisneros, to approve the Consent
Calendar.  Vote was unanimous.

MAYOR'S REPORTS AND PROCLAMATIONS:

Resolution of Commendation

Mayor Gonzales read in full a Resolution of Commendation on behalf of Eugene A.
Helsley, Director of Finance, who was recently awarded the professional credential of Certified Municipal Finance Administrator from the Municipal Treasurers
Association of United State and Canada, which was presented to Mr. Helsley along
with a framed certificate.

PUBLIC HEARINGS:

Personnel
Finance Department

Mayor Gonzales advised that Staff has requested the Public Hearing item, concerning
amending the Pay and Classification Plan for the City, be withdrawn from the Agenda,
for discussion with the employees' associations.

Appeals
Colton Iron & Metal

Mayor Gonzales referred to a letter dated September 18, 1979, from Don C. Bickley,
Attorney at Law, representing Colton Iron and Metal, requesting removal of the
Appeal matter from the Agenda.

EXECUTIVE SESSION:

The City Council adjourned to Executive Session at 7:43 p.m., to discuss matters
of Personnel, and reconvened at 8:25 p.m., all Members present.

RECESS:

The City Council recessed the meeting at 8:26 p.m., to conduct the business of
the Redevelopment Agency for the City of Colton, and reconvened at 8:40 p.m.

CORRESPONDENCE:

Luque Community Center

Mayor Gonzales read a letter dated September 17, 1979, from Father Gustavo Benson
of San Salvador Church, requesting the use of the Council Chambers and the Peter
Luque Community Center for a period of two months, beginning October 1, 1979, from
7:00 to 8:00 p.m., for religious education classes while the church classrooms
are being completed.

Moved by Councilwoman Cisneros, seconded by Councilman Hayes, approving request of
San Salvador Church for use of the Council Chambers and the Peter Luque Community
Center for religious education classes as outlined. Vote was unanimous.

CITY ATTORNEY'S REPORTS AND RECOMMENDATIONS:

Mayor Gonzales stated the City Attorney would like to take care of Item No. 20, concerning second reading of Ordinance No. 1568, prior to discussing Planning Commission
Item No. 10.

City Attorney Edwards introduced Ordinance No. 1568 for Second Reading, reading it
as to number and title only:

- 2 -

ORDINANCE NO. 1568 - AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY OF COLTON AMENDING ORDINANCE NO. 964,
(Second Reading)     AS AMENDED, AND PROVIDING FOR CERTAIN ZONE RECLASSIFICATIONS.

Moved by Councilwoman Cisneros to pass Second Reading and waive further reading of
Ordinance No. 1568.  Motion seconded by Councilman Rehrer and carried unanimously.

Moved by Councilwoman Cisneros, seconded by Councilman Hayes, to adopt Ordinance
No. 1568.  Vote was unanimous.

PLANNING COMMISSION REPORTS AND RECOMMENDATIONS:

Conditional Use Permits
    P. C. Res. No. 927

Planning Director Gonzales stated this item concerns a Conditional Use Permit ap-
plication requested by Walter L. McCubbin, to develop 44 condominium units, includ-
ing landscaping, recreational facilities and swimming pool, guest parking, and
ample open space, on approximately 4.8 acres of currently vacant land located south
of Cahuilla Street, between Cindee Lane and Mohave Drive.

Mr. Gonzales advised a recent General Plan amendment redesignated the subject
property from low density residential to medium density residential, 6 to 15
units per acre, with the proposed 44-unit condominium project on approximately
4.8 acres having a density of 9.16 units per acre, which would be consistent with
the present General Plan designation.  Mr. Gonzales further advised the subject
property is in the process of a change of zone, from R-3 to R-3-X, which would
make the proposed project consistent with the R-3-X zone.

Mr. Gonzales concluded the Planning Commission reviewed the application at a
public hearing held August 28, 1979, and recommend approval of the Conditional
Use Permit, subject to the findings and 15 conditions, as listed in Planning
Commission Resolution No. 927.

Moved by Councilman Rehrer, seconded by Councilman Temby, to approve Planning Com-
mission Resolution No. 927.  Unanimous vote.

Recreation
  Park Site

Mr. Gonzales reported from his memorandum dated September 12, 1979, regarding a
suitable location for the proposed tennis court, offered by the California Port-
land Cement Company, and the possibility of future land acquisition for a park
site and recreational facilities.

Mr. Gonzales said Staff recommends the City Council consider acquiring a recently
annexed parcel of land containing approximately 10 acres located north of Davis
Park and Paul J. Rogers Elementary School for a future park site, which could ac-
commodate the tennis court, future courts, baseball-soccer fields, swimming pool,
etc.  The property could be purchased with Park and Recreation Construction Tax
monies, the current fund containing approximately $208,777.42.  The property would
have to be appraised and purchased under threat of condemnation.

Moved by Councilwoman Cisneros, seconded by Councilman Hayes, approving Staff's
recommendation to purchase recently annexed land for future park site and recrea-
tional facilities.  Unanimous vote.

                                    - 3 -

Redevelopment
Res. No. 3936

Mr. Gonzales stated in accordance with resolutions adopted previously, during the
Redevelopment Agency Meeting, Staff was recommending approval of Resolution No.
3936, concerning the continuance of a public hearing between the City Council and
the Redevelopment Agency for the City of Colton to Tuesday, November 6, 1979.

Moved by Councilman Hayes, seconded by Councilman Rehrer, to approve Resolution
No. 3936. Unanimous vote.

RESOLUTION NO. 3936 - RESOLUTION OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF COLTON ACKNOWLEDGING RECEIPT OF RESOLUTIONS OF THE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY
FOR THE CITY OF COLTON REGARDING AMENDMENT NO. 2 TO THE REDEVELOPMENT PLAN FOR THE COOLEY RANCH, PROJECT NO. 4, CONTINUING A JOINT PUBLIC HEARING THEREON BY THE AGENCY AND THE
CITY COUNCIL, CONTINUING THE DATE FOR SUCH HEARING AND PROVIDING FOR THE GIVING OF NOTICE THEREOF.

RECREATION AND PARKS COMMISSION REPORTS AND RECOMMENDATIONS:

Budget
Recreation

Recreation Director Max Lofy read in full his report dated September 11, 1979, regarding budgeted funds for the Special Recreation Programs and the progress that
has been made thus far.

CITY MANAGER'S REPORTS AND RECOMMENDATIONS:

Appeals
Civil Service Board

Acting City Manager Elliott stated on August 15, 1979, the Civil Service Board held
a special meeting to consider the Appeal of Jerry Young. The findings have been
filed and certified copies have been delivered to the Mayor and City Council, as
per Rule XV, Section 5, of the City's Rules and Regulations.

Mr. Elliott said this matter is being continued from a previous Council Meeting
at the request of Mr. Young.

Mayor Gonzales stated the City Council had requested the Civil Service Board to
hold a hearing and make recommendations as to the discipline imposed on Jerry
Young, and the Board did hold such a hearing. The findings of the Board were
that the conduct of Mr. Young was insubordinate, that discipline was justified,
and the termination was reasonable.

Mayor Gonzales advised that the City Council has received a petition containing
approximately 203 signatures in support of Mr. Young, which he presented to
City Clerk Ramos for the record.

Mayor Gonzales asked Mr. Young if he wished to make a statement.

Mr. Young replied from the audience that he had no comments to make.

- 4 -

Councilman Rehrer stated that although he felt the Civil Service Board had done
a tremendous job in considering this matter, he did not go along with the Board's
decision, because there were many discrepancies involved since that time, and he
did not feel that Mr. Young should be penalized, therefore, he would make a motion
to reinstate Mr. Young to the position of City Auditor under the direction of the
City Manager's Office.

Motion seconded by Councilwoman Cisneros.

Mayor Gonzales stated that before the vote, he would like to clarify the validity
of the motion with the City Attorney, inasmuch as the position of City Auditor
had been deleted from the budget.

Councilman Rehrer questioned when the position had been deleted, and contended the
position of City Auditor was still on the books and a part of the Table of Organi-
zation.

City Attorney Edwards reviewed portions of the City Manager's Ordinance relating
to the powers and duties of the City Manager as Personnel Officer concerning per-
sonnel matters. Mr. Edwards advised that during the last Budget Session, the
position of City Auditor had been dropped from the proposed City Manager's Office
budget, and this was decided by the City Council.

Councilman Rehrer contended although this may have been deleted from the City
Manager's budget, the position itself, of City Auditor, had not been dropped
and was still on the books.

Acting City Manager Elliott stated, as a point of clarification, the position of
Auditor/Analyst is still a part of the Table of Organization, but not the position
of City Auditor.

At this point, Finance Director Helsey was asked to get copies of the adopted
1979-80 Budget to clarify the matter.

Mayor Gonzales read a portion of the Budget Report, indicating that the position
of City Auditor had been deleted, and the position of Auditor/Analyst would be
changed to Accounting Supervisor, pending a public hearing for this change.

Discussion then followed between Mayor Gonzales and Councilman Rehrer with regard
to the position of City Auditor.

Councilman Rehrer stated there is a motion and second on the floor, and called for
the question. Upon Roll Call, the Vote was:

AYES:     Three; Cisneros, Rehrer, Temby.
NOES:     Two; Gonzales, Hayes.
ABSENT:   None.
Motion carried.

Mr. Elliott requested Council direction on the funding of the position City Auditor.

Councilman Rehrer moved that monies be allocated from the Unappropriated Funds Ac-
count to cover the salary of City Auditor.

Councilman Temby stated he would second the motion, and would also like to direct

- 5 -

Staff to make a study of other vacant positions that have never been filled,
within the City's Table of Organization, and see where the City stands financially.

Mayor Gonzales said, in other words, the Council intends to allocate funds from
the City's Surplus Account to fund that position.

Councilwoman Cisneros stated that inasmuch as the position of Auditor/Analyst is
still a part of the Finance Department budget, why can't Mr. Young's salary be
paid with the funds from that position.

After additional discussion, Mayor Gonzales asked for a Roll Call Vote on the
motion as made:

AYES:      Three; Cisneros, Rehrer, Temby.
NOES:      Two; Gonzales, Hayes.
ABSENT:    None.
Motion carried.

Mr. Elliott then asked for a reinstatement date.

Moved by Councilman Rehrer, seconded by Councilwoman Cisneros, to reinstate Mr.
Young effective from the date of termination, with no loss in pay. Upon Roll
Call, the Vote was:

AYES:      Three; Cisneros, Rehrer, Temby.
NOES:      Two; Gonzales, Hayes.
ABSENT:    None.
Motion carried.

Mayor Gonzales stated he was opposed to the reinstatement of Mr. Young because
of the decision and recommendation of the Civil Service Board which was outlined
in the verbatim transcript of the board hearing and made available to each Council
Member. Mayor Gonzales also commented on a meeting held by a group of citizens,
attended by Councilmen Rehrer and Temby, apparently to discuss the reinstatement
of Mr. Young, which he felt showed unprofessionalism on the part of the two Councilmen. Mayor Gonzales said Councilman Temby should have abstained from voting on
this matter because of the conflict of interest created by a political campaign
contribution from Mr. Young to Mr. Temby.

Councilman Rehrer stated he had attended a meeting with interested citizens of
the community to discuss City issues in general, however, Councilman Temby was
not present at the meeting. Mr. Rehrer said the people in attendance at this
meeting have never donated to his cause or campaigns and he was not pressured
in any way in reaching the decision he made this evening.

Chamber of Commerce
Appropriations

Mr. Elliott referred to a letter from the Chamber of Commerce requesting the
City Council allocate an amount of $600 to be used toward the printing of their
1979-80 Economic Profile for the City, promotional material.

Moved by Councilwoman Cisneros, seconded by Councilman Temby, to approve and allocate from the Community Promotions Fund the amount of $600 for the Chamber of
Commerce as requested. Motion carried, with Mayor Gonzales voting NO.

- 6 -

Swimming Pool Report

Mr. Elliott stated the Council Members have received copies of the swimming pool
progress report from Director of Building and Safety John Twiss, dated September
13, 1979, which is an informational item.

Personnel
Planning

Mr. Elliott advised the Planning Department is requesting that the job description
for Planning Associate be amended in the areas of qualifications, education and
experience, as the majority of the applicants did not meet the minimum qualifica-
tions for the position.

Moved by Councilman Hayes, seconded by Councilman Rehrer, to approve amendment
to Job Description for Planning Associate as requested. Vote was unanimous.

Fees
Cemetery

Mr. Elliott reported from a memorandum dated September 11, 1979, regarding an in-
crease in the price for various items at the Cemetery, including flower vases,
sectional box liners, vault, and transfer fee, to be effective October 1, 1979.

Mr. Elliott said Staff recommends approval of Resolution No. 3937 establishing
the increase in these fees.

Moved by Councilman Hayes, seconded by Councilman Rehrer, to approve Resolution
No. 3937. Motion carried, with Mayor Gonzales voting NO.

RESOLUTION NO. 3937 - RESOLUTION OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF COLTON ESTAB-
LISHING SCHEDULE OF FEES FOR CEMETERY ITEMS AND SERVICES.

CITY ATTORNEY'S REPORTS AND RECOMMENDATIONS:

Ordinance No. 1570
First Reading

City Attorney Edwards introduced Ordinance No. 1570 for First Reading, reading it as
to number and title only, stating this ordinance amends Ordinance No. 1405, regard-
ing cable TV franchises.

ORDINANCE NO. 1570 - AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY OF COLTON, CALIFORNIA, AMENDING COLTON
(First Reading)      CITY CODE CONCERNING THE REGULATION OF CABLE TELEVISION.

Moved by Councilwoman Cisneros, seconded by Councilman Hayes, to pass first reading
of Ordinance No. 1570, and waive further reading. Unanimous vote.

Ordinance No. 1569
Second Reading

City Attorney Edwards introduced Ordinance No. 1569 for second reading, reading it
as to number and title only:

- 7 -

ORDINANCE NO. 1569 - AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF COLTON AMEND-
(Second Reading)        ING ORDINANCE NO. 1450.

Moved by Councilman Rehrer, seconded by Councilman Hayes, to pass second reading of
Ordinance No. 1569, and waive further reading. Unanimous vote.

Moved by Councilwoman Cisneros, seconded by Councilman Rehrer, to adopt Ordinance
No. 1569. Vote was unanimous.

ORAL COMMUNICATIONS:

Mr. Gene McMeans, 12004 Glenwood Avenue, Colton, stated he would like to publicly
commend and thank the Colton Fire Department for their quick action and invaluable
assistance during the recent fire in Reche Canyon. Mr. McMeans said that he, as
well as so many others in the Canyon, really appreciate their service.

Mrs. Pauline Garcia, 489 North 4th Street, stated she would like to speak to the
City Council with regard to item No. 14, and the reinstatement of Mr. Young.

Mrs. Garcia said she is very upset with the action taken by the City Council this
evening. Mrs. Garcia continued first the City Manager is forced to resign, causing
demoralization among department heads; then a department head's recommendation, a long
with that of our Civil Service Board, which was arrived at in a public hearing, are
completely ignored, and an employee who is ruled incapable and insubordinate is rein-
stated at the expense of our taxpayers. Mrs. Garcia said the City Council is remiss
in their duties to the community.

Mrs. Garcia also stated that Councilman Temby was guilty of a conflict of interest
in voting on two occasions regarding Mr. Young's appeal, because he had accepted
a political campaign contribution from Mr. Young and, therefore, should have ab-
stained from voting.

Councilman Temby then asked the City Attorney if there was any conflict of interest.

City Attorney Edwards responded no, there was not.

RESOLUTION NO. 3650

RESOLUTION OF THE COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF COLTON, AMEND-
ING RESOLUTION NO. 3623, ADOPTING A CONFLICT OF INTEREST
CODE APPLICABLE TO THE MAYOR, MEMBERS OF THE CITY COUNCIL
AND THE CITY MANAGER, PURSUANT TO THE POLITICAL REFORM
ACT OF 1974.

THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF COLTON HEREBY RESOLVES AS FOLLOWS:

SECTION 1. City Council Resolution No. 3623 is hereby amended to
read as follows:

RESOLUTION NO. 3623

RESOLUTION OF THE COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF COLTON
ADOPTING A CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODE APPLICABLE
TO MEMBERS OF THE CITY COUNCIL PURSUANT TO THE
POLITICAL REFORM ACT OF 1974.

Section 1. ADOPTION OF CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODE. In compliance
with Section 87300 of the Government Code, the City Council hereby adopts this
Conflict of Interest Code.

Section 2. APPLICATION OF CODE. This Conflict of Interest Code
shall be applicable to members of the City Council, whether acting as a Council
member or as governing board member or commissioner of any city agency.

Section 3. DISCLOSURE. Councilmembers are already required to
disclose investments, interest in real property and income under Sections 87200
and the following of the Government Code. Therefore, no other or additional
disclosure requirements are imposed by this Conflict of Interest Code.

Section 4. CIRCUMSTANCES REQUIRING DISQUALIFICATION. Any member
of the City Council, whether acting as a Council member or as governing board
member or commissioner of any city agency, must disqualify himself or herself
from making or participating in the making of any decisions which will fore-
seeably have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on
the public generally, on any economic interest, as defined in Government Code
Section 87103. No member shall be prevented from making or participating in

che making of any decision to the extent his or her participation is legally

required for the decision to be made.

Section 5. DEFINITIONS. Except as otherwise indicated, the

definitions contained in the Political Reform Act of 1974 (Government Code

Section 81000) and Regulations adopted pursuant thereto are incorporated into

this Conflict of Interest Code.

SECTION 2. The City Clerk is directed to forward a certified copy of

this resolution to the Fair Political Practices Commission.

SECTION 3. The City Clerk shall certify to the adoption of this

resolution as prescribed by law.

PASSED, ADOPTED and APPROVED this 7th day of December, 1976.


ABE E. BELTRAN
Mayor of the City of Colton, California

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Councilman Leonard Temby must disqualify himself from voting on personnel matters affecting employee Jerry Young, who had contributed to Temby's 1978 campaign committee.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Campaign contributions are statutorily excluded from the definition of 'income' under the Political Reform Act, so the councilmember is not required to disqualify himself based on the Act's conflict of interest provisions. However, the FPPC notes that California courts are considering whether such participation creates an appearance of bias.

BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest (916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444 October 19, 1979 Honorable Frank A. Gonzales, Mayor City of Colton Colton Civic Center 650 North La Cadena Drive Colton, CA 92324 Dear Mayor Gonzales: In your letter of September 28, 1979, you inquire about a matter arising under the disqualification provisions (Government Code Sections 87100, et seq.) of the Political Reform Act ("the Act"). Because, under the Act, opinions and advice are issued only in response to inquiries from persons, or on behalf of persons, whose duties are the subject of the inquiries, your request for a formal opinion in this matter is denied. The following discussion is provided for information only. You ask specifically whether a city councilmember should be disqualified from participation in city council personnel actions concerning a discharged city employee who had contributed to the councilmember's 1978 campaign committee. Government Code Section 87100 prohibits any public official at any level of state or local government from making, participating in the making or in any way attempting to use his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest. Government Code Section 87103 states that a public official has "financial interest" in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on: "(c) any source of income...." The terms of Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103 are substantially incorporated by Section 4 of City of Colton Resolution No. 3623, adopting a conflict of interest code applicable to members of the city council pursuant to the Political Reform Act of 1974. Honorable Frank A. Gonzales, Mayor October 19, 1979 Page Two "Income" is specifically defined by Government Code Section 82030(b)(1) to exclude campaign contributions required to be reported under Chapter 4 of the Act. Assuming that the employee's contribution to the council- man is a contribution within the meaning of the Act, it would not constitute "income" to the councilmember. Accordingly, the Act would not require the councilmember to disqualify himself from participating in decisions affecting the employee. Even though the councilmember would not be required to disqualify himself under the Act, please be advised that the Supreme Court is presently considering the question whether city councilmembers' participation in a zoning decision directly affecting contributors to their campaigns may be said to create an appearance of bias sufficient to provide a basis for overturning a decision under Code of Civil Procedure 1094.5. Woodland Hills Residents Ass'n., Inc., et al. v. Los Angeles City Council (L.A. No. 31133). Very truly yours, [signature] Thomas M. Sobel Chief, Legal Division TMS:plh CITY OF COLTON SEPTEMBER 28, 1979 Fair Political Practices Commission 1100 Kay Street Sacramento,
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to Mayor Frank Gonzales of Colton regarding whether Councilman Temby should disqualify himself from voting on personnel matters affecting employee Jerry Young, a campaign contributor. The FPPC concludes that campaign contributions are excluded from the statutory definition of income, so disqualification is not required under the Political Reform Act, but notes pending Supreme Court consideration of appearance of bias issues.
